target.title: Add Telegram reliability and handoff commands
target.body:
## Summary
- add `/handoff`, `/resume latest`, and related handoff commands for explicit session handoff text
- add lightweight Telegram inflight state tracking for dispatched/completed/failed/interrupted requests
- send explicit user-facing failure notices for timeout/provider/context/session-lock/delivery failures
- guard very long Telegram inputs when the current session is already high-context
- mark stale inflight requests as interrupted on Telegram runtime startup and notify the original chat without auto-replay
- document the command surface, changelog entry, and conservative rollout/rollback runbook

## Safety boundaries
- Draft PR only; do not deploy directly to production
- does not restart the gateway
- does not retry or replay interrupted requests
- does not cancel or clean tasks
- does not change models, bindings, config, secrets, or sessions
- does not call tasks audit/show from Telegram hot paths
- `/new` alone still starts fresh; handoff is opt-in via `/handoff` then `/resume latest`

## Tests
- `git diff --check`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-handoff.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm build`

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram operator messages can currently appear to disappear when gateway/model/session/delivery work is interrupted, and long Telegram sessions require manual handoff text. This patch adds explicit handoff commands, Telegram inflight lifecycle recording, failure/interrupted notifications, and high-context long-input protection.
- **Real environment tested**: Shadow OpenClaw source install on Windows, isolated from production with `--profile codex-shadow-telegram-reliability-token`, token auth, and non-production loopback port `19790`. The shadow profile wrote state under `C:\Users\lcxhh\.openclaw-codex-shadow-telegram-reliability-token`; production OpenClaw config, secrets, sessions, bindings, models, and gateway service were not replaced or restarted.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  - `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
  - `corepack pnpm build`
  - `node openclaw.mjs --version`
  - `node openclaw.mjs --profile codex-shadow-telegram-reliability-token gateway run --dev --port 19790 --auth token --token [shadow-token] --bind loopback --ws-log compact`
  - `node openclaw.mjs --profile codex-shadow-telegram-reliability-token gateway probe --url ws://127.0.0.1:19790 --timeout 10000 --token [shadow-token] --json`
  - `node openclaw.mjs --profile codex-shadow-telegram-reliability-token gateway health --url ws://127.0.0.1:19790 --timeout 10000 --token [shadow-token] --json`
  - `node openclaw.mjs --profile codex-shadow-telegram-reliability-token gateway status --url ws://127.0.0.1:19790 --timeout 10000 --token [shadow-token] --json`
- **Evidence after fix**: `node openclaw.mjs --version` reported `OpenClaw 2026.5.4 (6c138ab)`. The shadow gateway logged `http server listening` and `ready` on port `19790`. The second shadow `probe` returned `ok: true`, `degraded: false`, `capability: read_only`, `connect.ok: true`, `rpcOk: true`, and `scopes: [operator.read]`. `health` returned `ok: true`; `status` returned `rpc.ok: true` with `url: ws://127.0.0.1:19790`. The shadow process was then stopped and did not remain running.
- **Observed result after fix**: The patched source builds and can start a separate, read-probeable Gateway from a shadow profile without touching production. Targeted tests validate the new handoff, dispatch, inflight, and failure-notice paths: handoff command tests 8/8 passed; Telegram dispatch/bot tests 194/194 passed.
- **What was not tested**: No production deployment, no global package replacement, no production gateway restart, no live Telegram bot token/delivery replacement, no task cleanup, no model/binding/config/secrets/session changes. Live Telegram delivery proof is still intentionally deferred until maintainer review approves a rollout window.

target.title: fix(logs): only report rotation when the log file actually shrank
target.body:
**PR title:** `fix(logs): only report rotation when the log file actually shrank`

---

## Summary

- **Problem:** `openclaw logs --follow` prints `Log cursor reset (file rotated).` to stderr whenever the gateway log grows faster than `--max-bytes` between polls, even though no rotation has occurred ŌĆö the cursor is still valid and the tail was just truncated to fit the byte budget.
- **Why it matters:** Operators watching busy gateways see false "file rotated" notices on every burst (e.g. during a noisy startup or while a chatty channel pumps logs). The accompanying `Log tail truncated (increase --max-bytes).` line is the correct signal; the rotation line muddies it and trains readers to ignore both.
- **What changed:** In `src/logging/log-tail.ts::readLogSlice`, only set `reset = true` in the true rotation case (`cursor > size` ŌĆö the file shrank below the previous cursor). The fast-growth branch (`size - start > maxBytes`) now sets `truncated = true` only, since the cursor it returned remains contiguous with what the caller saw last poll.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** The `LogTailPayload` shape, the rotation-case behavior, the `Log tail truncated` message, and the byte-budget arithmetic are all unchanged. No CLI strings, schema, or downstream consumers were modified ŌĆö `src/logging/diagnostic-support-export.ts` and `src/cli/logs-cli.ts` continue to work with the same fields.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration *(logging is shared infra)*
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX *(stderr notice text)*
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- No existing issue. Found while reading sibling cron handler code around #74066.
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- **Root cause:** `readLogSlice` conflated two situations into a single `reset` flag:
  1. `cursor > size` ŌĆö the previously-returned cursor is past EOF; the file was rotated/truncated, so the cursor is meaningless and we must restart from the new tail.
  2. `size - start > maxBytes` ŌĆö the file grew faster than the per-poll byte budget; the cursor is still valid, we just can't read everything that was appended in one poll.
  Only case (1) is a real reset/rotation. Case (2) was incorrectly being flagged as "rotated" because both branches set `reset = true`.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** `log-tail.test.ts` only covered the redaction path; nothing pinned the meaning of `reset` vs `truncated`.
- **Contributing context:** The CLI side (`src/cli/logs-cli.ts:402`) hard-codes `Log cursor reset (file rotated).` for `payload.reset`, so the conflation surfaced as a misleading user-visible string rather than a silent boolean.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file:** `src/logging/log-tail.test.ts`
- **Scenarios the test locks in:**
  1. File grows by `>> maxBytes` between two polls with the same valid cursor ŌåÆ `truncated: true`, `reset: false`, lines returned.
  2. File shrinks below the previous cursor (real rotation) ŌåÆ `reset: true`.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** the bug is at the `readLogSlice` boundary; both scenarios can be exercised end-to-end against a real temp log file using the existing `setLoggerOverride` test seam.
- **Existing test that already covers this (if any):** none.
- **If no new test is added, why not:** N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`openclaw logs --follow` (and JSON-mode `--json`) no longer emits the `Log cursor reset (file rotated).` notice during high-throughput writes. The existing `Log tail truncated (increase --max-bytes).` notice still fires in that case, which is the actionable signal. The notice still fires correctly when the log is genuinely rotated/truncated.

## Diagram (if applicable)

`N/A`

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu 24.04 (WSL) / Windows 11 host
- Runtime/container: Node 22 (`pnpm dev`)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A ŌĆö gateway logs only
- Relevant config (redacted): default loggerSettings

### Steps (to reproduce the original misleading notice on `main`)

1. Start a gateway with anything that writes a steady stream of log lines (e.g. a chatty channel or `openclaw status --watch` against a busy gateway).
2. In another terminal: `openclaw logs --follow --max-bytes 4000`.
3. Wait for a write burst that exceeds 4 KB between two polls.

### Expected

Only `Log tail truncated (increase --max-bytes).` is printed to stderr while the burst is being caught up.

### Actual (before this PR)

Both `Log tail truncated (increase --max-bytes).` **and** `Log cursor reset (file rotated).` are printed, even though the file was never rotated.

### After this PR

Only the truthful `Log tail truncated` notice is printed during fast growth. The rotation notice still fires when the file is actually rotated/truncated (covered by the second new test).

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after *(see new tests in `src/logging/log-tail.test.ts`)*
- [x] Trace/log snippets *(see "Repro + Verification")*
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Local verification:

```
# Targeted unit lane
pnpm test src/logging/log-tail.test.ts
# Expect: 3 tests pass (1 existing + 2 new)
```

> Note: I was unable to run the full repository test sweep locally (the dev environment is on a WSL/Windows-mounted filesystem that pnpm refuses to install into with `ERR_PNPM_EACCES`). The fix is a 5-line local change inside `readLogSlice` with no schema, no public-API, and no other call-site impact, and the new tests exercise the exact two paths affected. CI in this repo runs the full lane.

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios:** fast file growth between polls ŌåÆ no rotation message; real rotation (file shrunk) ŌåÆ rotation message still emitted.
- **Edge cases checked:** initial poll with no cursor still uses `start = max(0, size - maxBytes)` and reports `truncated` only (unchanged); empty file still returns `cursor: size, lines: []` (unchanged); cursor inside `[start, size]` window with `size - start <= maxBytes` is unchanged.
- **What I did NOT verify:** live CLI output against a running daemon during a real burst (will rely on CI + reviewer spot-check).

## Review Conversations

- [x] I will reply to or resolve every bot review conversation I address in this PR.
- [x] I will leave unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`
- N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** a downstream consumer relies on `reset === true` to mean "skipped some lines" rather than "the cursor is no longer meaningful".
  - **Mitigation:** I grepped the repo for `payload.reset` / `LogTailPayload`. The only consumers are `src/cli/logs-cli.ts` (renders the user-visible string this PR is correcting) and `src/logging/diagnostic-support-export.ts::sanitizeLogTail` (passes the boolean through verbatim into the support bundle). Both are display-only; neither uses `reset` to decide whether lines were skipped ŌĆö they use the existing `truncated` flag for that.

---

## AI/Vibe-Coded PRs Welcome! ­¤ż¢

- [x] **AI-assisted PR.** Drafted with help from Claude (Opus 4.7) via the GitHub Copilot CLI agent harness.
- [x] **Degree of testing:** lightly tested ŌĆö new unit tests added; full repo lane not run locally (see note above).
- [x] **Prompts/session logs:** available on request.
- [x] I understand exactly what the code does (5-line semantic narrowing of when `reset` is asserted in `readLogSlice`).
- [ ] `codex review --base origin/main` not run locally (no Codex access in this environment); happy to address Codex bot comments after the PR opens.
- [x] Will resolve bot review conversations after addressing them.



## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** `readLogSlice` in `src/logging/log-tail.ts` raised the `reset` flag (rendered by the CLI as `Log cursor reset (file rotated).`) whenever the log grew faster than `--max-bytes` between two polls, even though no rotation happened ŌĆö the cursor was still valid and only the tail was byte-budget truncated. Operators watching a busy gateway saw false "file rotated" notices on every burst.

**Real environment tested:** Local Node v22 runtime (Ubuntu 24.04 / WSL) driving the actual shipped `readConfiguredLogTail` export from this branch through `node --import tsx`, pointed at a real on-disk log file via the public `setLoggerOverride({ file })` seam. The harness writes, appends to, and rotates a genuine temp log file on disk and reads it back through the real code path ŌĆö no fakes for the slice logic.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```
node --import tsx ./proof_logtail.ts
// proof_logtail.ts seeds a real temp log file, reads it once to get a cursor,
// appends ~400 lines (far beyond maxBytes=4000) to the SAME file (fast growth,
// no rotation), then truncates the file below the cursor (real rotation), reading
// the real readConfiguredLogTail payload at each step. Run against upstream/main and this branch.
```

**Evidence after fix:** Real console output captured from the command above, run first against the current upstream/main slice logic, then against this branch:
```
########## BEFORE PATCH (current upstream/main log-tail.ts) ##########
Poll 1 (seed) -> cursor: 54 reset: false truncated: false
Poll 2 (fast growth) -> reset: true truncated: true lines: 85
Poll 3 (real rotation) -> reset: true truncated: false lines: 1

########## AFTER PATCH (this branch log-tail.ts) ##########
Poll 1 (seed) -> cursor: 54 reset: false truncated: false
Poll 2 (fast growth) -> reset: false truncated: true lines: 85
Poll 3 (real rotation) -> reset: true truncated: false lines: 1
```

**Observed result after fix:** Before the change, Poll 2 (fast growth, same file) reported `reset: true` ŌĆö a false rotation signal. After the change the identical real scenario reports `reset: false truncated: true`, while Poll 3 (the file genuinely shrinking below the cursor) still correctly reports `reset: true`. The misleading rotation notice is gone for fast growth and preserved for real rotation.

**What was not tested:** Did not drive the `openclaw logs --follow` CLI presentation layer against a live long-running daemon during an organic write burst; the proof targets `readLogSlice` where the flag is set. The CLI string mapping of `reset` is unchanged by this PR.

target.title: fix: log and inject synthetic error results for unknown tool calls
target.body:
## Summary

  - Add WARN logging when unknown/invalid tool calls are silently dropped by the session guard,
   recording tool name, ID, and drop reason
  - Inject synthetic `isError: true` toolResult into the transcript so the model receives
  explicit feedback ("Tool X is not available") and can self-correct instead of repeating the
  same invalid call
  - Export `sanitizeToolCallInputsWithReport()` and `DroppedToolCallInfo` for structured
  diagnostics

  ## Problem

  When the model returns a tool call not in `allowedToolNames`, `guardedAppend()` in
  `session-tool-result-guard.ts` silently drops the entire assistant message with **no log and
  no feedback to the model**. This causes:

  1. **No observability** ŌĆö operators have no way to detect misconfigured tool lists or model
  hallucinations
  2. **Repeated API waste** ŌĆö the model never learns the tool is unavailable and may repeat the
   same call
  3. **Cost risk** ŌĆö worst case up to `MAX_RUN_LOOP_ITERATIONS` (32ŌĆō160) wasted API calls per
  request


  ## Changes

  | File | What changed |
  |------|-------------|
  | `src/agents/session-transcript-repair.ts` | `repairToolCallInputs()` now collects
  `DroppedToolCallInfo[]` with per-call drop reason; new `sanitizeToolCallInputsWithReport()`
  export |
  | `src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.ts` | `guardedAppend()` calls
  `sanitizeToolCallInputsWithReport()`, emits WARN log on drops, injects synthetic error
  toolResult per dropped call via `makeUnknownToolErrorResult()` |
  | `src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts` | Updated 3 existing tests + added 2 new
  tests verifying synthetic error injection for malformed calls, unknown tools, and mixed
  valid/invalid messages |

  ## Test plan

  - [x] Existing tests updated: malformed (missing input), invalid name tokens,
  not-in-allowlist now assert synthetic error result
  - [x] New test: mixed message with valid `read` + unknown `unknown_tool` ŌĆö valid call
  preserved, error injected for unknown
  - [x] New test: standalone malformed call (missing input) gets synthetic error
  - [ ] `pnpm test src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts`
  - [ ] `pnpm check:changed`

target.title: fix(media): include configured roots in path-not-allowed error hint
target.body:
## Problem

`assertLocalMediaAllowed` rejects out-of-tree paths with:

```
Local media path is not under an allowed directory: /tmp/file.png
```

This tells the operator their path was wrong but not what *was* allowed. In production the user just sees the rejection; they have no way to tell whether `/tmp` was excluded, what `/root/.openclaw/media/inbound` looks like, or why moving the file would fix it. The result is "image tool failed ŌåÆ I have no idea where to put the file".

This shows up in real workflows where users (or other tools) drop files in `/tmp` thinking it's a generally accessible scratch dir, then hit the rejection without any hint that the runtime expects paths under the configured local roots.

## Fix

Append a short `(allowed roots: ŌĆ”)` hint to the `path-not-allowed` error message, listing the first 4 configured roots and a `+N more` overflow marker for the rest. The format is:

```
Local media path is not under an allowed directory: /tmp/file.png \
  (allowed roots: /root/.openclaw/workspace, /root/.openclaw/media/inbound, /root/.openclaw/media/outbound)
```

ŌĆ”and for misconfigured configs with many roots:

```
Local media path is not under an allowed directory: /var/elsewhere/file.png \
  (allowed roots: /tmp/openclaw-1, /tmp/openclaw-2, /tmp/openclaw-3, /tmp/openclaw-4, +3 more)
```

The 4-root cap (`ALLOWED_ROOTS_HINT_LIMIT`) keeps the error message bounded even when a config drifts large; an overflow marker with the elided count keeps the operator aware that the list is non-trivial. The message still surfaces the rejected path (unchanged contract).

Both throw sites share the new `formatAllowedRootsHint` helper so the workspace-fast-path branch and the general-loop branch produce identical error shapes.

## Tests

Two new regression cases in `src/media/local-media-access.test.ts`:

- A small allowed-roots list shows up verbatim in the rejection error and the rejected path is preserved.
- A long allowed-roots list (> 4 entries) is capped, the first roots inline, the trailing roots collapsed into a `+N more` marker, and the cap-ed entries do **not** appear verbatim in the message.

Existing tests (managed inbound media bypass, nested inbound rejection) still pass ŌĆö the hint is purely additive on the existing `path-not-allowed` error.

`pnpm exec vitest run src/media/local-media-access.test.ts` is green locally (4/4).

## Notes

- `LocalMediaAccessError.code` is unchanged (`"path-not-allowed"`), so any caller filtering on the error code keeps working. Only the human-readable message body grows.
- Showing the configured roots is acceptable here because the error is server-side: it goes back to the operator (who has filesystem access anyway) and the runtime already publishes the same paths in `openclaw status`, config docs, and similar diagnostics. No new leakage.
- The hint stays inside the same single-line message so logs that grep for "Local media path is not under an allowed directory" continue to match.

target.title: fix(agents): harden subagent lifecycle calls against transient gateway readiness failures
target.body:
## Summary

- add a bounded `sessions.list` readiness probe before subagent spawn starts the child agent
- retry transient finalize lifecycle calls in subagent announce (`sessions.patch` / `sessions.delete`)
- retry transient sweep cleanup `sessions.delete` calls in subagent registry cleanup
- restore the missing sweep delete helper wiring on current `main`
- restore/expand focused regression coverage for sweep cleanup and announce finalize retry paths
- keep the patch narrowed to the still-missing lifecycle hardening pieces on current `main`

## Why this refreshed PR

The earlier PR branch became stale relative to current `main` because related subagent delivery/orchestration code moved substantially. This refreshed branch keeps the same bug-fix intent, but re-applies only the still-missing resilience behavior on top of current `main` with a smaller diff.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Root Cause

Several subagent lifecycle paths still treat short gateway readiness/lifecycle gaps as single-shot failures:
- spawn starts the child agent immediately, without a bounded readiness preflight
- subagent announce finalize calls swallow transient lifecycle errors instead of retrying them
- registry sweep cleanup retries only on a later sweep, rather than immediately retrying known transient lifecycle failures in bounded fashion

## Test Plan

- `OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=tmp/pr-74131-final-verify.json node --no-maglev ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts`

## Results

- `src/agents/subagent-registry-cleanup.test.ts` Ō£ģ
- `src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts` Ō£ģ
- `src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts` Ō£ģ
- `src/agents/subagent-announce.test.ts` Ō£ģ
- 4/4 targeted files passed
- 41/41 targeted tests passed

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Subagent lifecycle calls can now tolerate transient gateway readiness/lifecycle failures around spawn, completion announce/finalize, and registry cleanup while keeping permanent failures non-retried.

Real environment tested: Repository `openclaw/openclaw`, PR #74131 worktree `/datad/github/openclaw-pr74131-followup`, local validation commit `62dbc3f15271`, PR head `f6511ea7426f` (`fix/subagent-gateway-readiness-refresh`), on Linux using Node.js v24.14.1. The only diff between the validated local commit and PR head was `CHANGELOG.md` release-note cleanup (3 deleted lines); source and test files were unchanged. To avoid disrupting the user's production gateway, I ran the PR build against a temporary loopback gateway on port 19131 with a temporary OpenClaw HOME/workspace under `/tmp/openclaw-pr74131-proof`; channels, cron, browser control, and canvas host were disabled. The temporary gateway reused only copied provider auth profile data converted into the temporary SQLite auth store. The production gateway on 18789 was not restarted or used for this proof and was checked healthy afterward.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```shell
# Start temporary PR gateway from the PR worktree.
OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-pr74131-proof/home OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr74131-proof/home/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr74131-proof/home OPENCLAW_WORKSPACE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr74131-proof/workspace OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_CRON=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_BROWSER_CONTROL_SERVER=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_CANVAS_HOST=1 node openclaw.mjs gateway run --port 19131 --bind loopback --auth token --token '<temp-token>' --verbose

# Run a live parent agent turn that must spawn a child, yield, receive the child completion event, then answer from the parent session.
OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-pr74131-proof/home OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr74131-proof/home/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr74131-proof/home OPENCLAW_WORKSPACE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr74131-proof/workspace OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_URL=ws://127.0.0.1:19131 OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN='<temp-token>' node openclaw.mjs agent --json   --session-key 'agent:main:pr74131-proof-1780818361-22239'   --timeout 360   --message 'Run this exact OpenClaw subagent lifecycle proof. Use tool calls, not prose.
Step 1: call sessions_spawn with task exactly: "Reply exactly PR74131_CHILD_OK_1780818361-22239 and nothing else." Use cleanup="keep" and context="isolated".
Step 2: after sessions_spawn returns accepted, call sessions_yield to wait for the child completion event.
Step 3: after the child completion event is visible, reply exactly PR74131_PARENT_SAW_1780818361-22239 and nothing else.
Do not reply with PR74131_PARENT_SAW_1780818361-22239 before the child completion event is visible.'

# Run targeted forced-transient lifecycle tests.
OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node scripts/test-projects.mjs   src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts   src/agents/subagent-announce.test.ts   src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts   src/agents/subagent-registry-helpers.test.ts   src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix: Redacted terminal/log evidence from the temporary PR gateway and targeted tests:
```text
2026-06-07T15:45:38.251+08:00 [gateway] ready
2026-06-07T15:46:31.898+08:00 [ws] ŌåÉ connect client=gateway-client clientDisplayName=gateway:sessions.list ... auth=token
2026-06-07T15:46:32.982+08:00 [ws] Ōćä res Ō£ō sessions.list 445ms
2026-06-07T15:46:35.568+08:00 [diagnostic] lane dequeue: lane=subagent waitMs=1 queueSize=0
2026-06-07T15:46:37.333+08:00 [ws] ŌåÆ event agent ... session=subagent:<uuid> stream=assistant ... text=PR74131_CHILD_OK_1780818361-22239
2026-06-07T15:46:37.337+08:00 [ws] ŌåÆ event agent ... session=subagent:<uuid> stream=lifecycle ... phase=end
2026-06-07T15:46:39.793+08:00 [agent/embedded] embedded run start: runId=announce:v1:agent:main:subagent:<uuid>:<uuid> ... provider=deepseek model=deepseek-v4-flash
2026-06-07T15:46:41.499+08:00 [agent/embedded] embedded run agent end: runId=announce:v1:agent:main:subagent:<uuid>:<uuid> isError=false
2026-06-07T15:46:41.568+08:00 [agent/embedded] embedded run done: runId=announce:v1:agent:main:subagent:<uuid>:<uuid> ... aborted=false
Parent trajectory assistantTexts: ["PR74131_PARENT_SAW_1780818361-22239"]

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts
Ō£ō agents src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts (60 tests)
Ō£ō agents src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts (23 tests)
Ō£ō agents src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts (98 tests)
Ō£ō agents src/agents/subagent-announce.test.ts (12 tests)
Ō£ō agents src/agents/subagent-registry-helpers.test.ts (8 tests)
  Ō£ō retries up to the limit on transient errors
  Ō£ō throws the last transient error after exhausting retries
  Ō£ō retries lifecycle readiness failures shared with subagent spawn
Test Files 5 passed (5)
Tests 201 passed (201)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 55.81s

Post-check: temporary port 19131 had no remaining listener; production gateway 18789 remained running and connectivity probe was ok.
```

Observed result after fix: The live parent session performed a real `sessions_spawn` call, the PR's admin-scoped `sessions.list` readiness probe succeeded before child spawn, the child subagent completed with the expected token, the completion announce resumed the parent session, and the parent replied only after observing the child completion event. The targeted test shard also passed all 201 tests covering transient retry behavior for spawn readiness, announce/finalize delivery, and registry cleanup helpers.

What was not tested: I did not deliberately restart or kill the user's production gateway. I also did not inject real network-level gateway outages in the live temporary gateway; transient failure branches were covered by the targeted tests, while the live run validated the after-fix real subagent spawn/completion/announce lifecycle on the PR code path.

## CI notes

- `Real behavior proof` check is now passing ŌĆö both live runtime proof and forced-transient lifecycle proof are in the PR body.
- No CHANGELOG entries in the PR diff ŌĆö release notes are maintainer-owned.
- Remaining CI failures (if any) are infrastructure issues (actionlint binary download, corepack config, `message-action-runner.core-send.test.ts` timeout) not caused by this PR's code changes.

## Notes

- Greptile / review findings about missing helper wiring and weakened cleanup coverage are addressed in this refresh.
- Added focused announce finalize retry coverage for both `sessions.delete` and `sessions.patch` transient failure paths.
- `subagent-announce` finalize timeout assertions were updated to match the current `SUBAGENT_ANNOUNCE_SESSION_FINALIZE_TIMEOUT_MS` behavior.
- P1 fix: `isGatewayLifecycleTransientError` now excludes permanent WS close codes (1008 Policy Violation, 1003, 1007) and explicit auth denials (unauthorized, forbidden, access denied) from retry ŌĆö these fail fast instead.
- P2 fix: CHANGELOG entries removed from PR branch.

## PR Self-check

- [x] helper definition/import/export chain re-checked
- [x] cleanup / finalize paths re-checked end-to-end
- [x] previously weakened regression assertion restored or replaced with equivalent protection
- [x] targeted tests added for the exact transient retry paths touched by this PR
- [x] targeted agents verification rerun after final edits

## Risks / Notes

- The lifecycle retry classifiers are still message-based, so future transient variants may need to be added explicitly.
- Retries are bounded and localized to the known gateway readiness/lifecycle paths.
- I have not treated full-repo heavy gates as complete for this patch; this update is grounded on focused path-level verification.

## Supersedes / Related

- Related to / refreshes the intent of #70434

target.title: fix(sandbox): pass --init so tini reaps zombie processes
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem:
What: Adding --init to the docker sandbox makes tini the PID 1 and reaps zombie processes.
Why: Long-running sandboxes generate a large number of short-lived child processes (shell/browser helpers), and the entrypoint does not reap them ŌåÆ <defunct> processes accumulate ŌåÆ kernel.pid_max is exhausted ŌåÆ subsequent fork/exec fails.
Evidence: A new test was added to assert that --init appears in the output of buildSandboxCreateArgs and is placed after create; all 17 local tests passed.

target.title: [codex] Route generated media through assistant delivery
target.body:
## Summary
- remove the async media completion direct-send path and its `tools.media.asyncCompletion.directSend` config surface
- keep async music/video completion as a requester-session wake so the assistant receives the generated paths and owns the follow-up delivery
- stop generated image/music/video/TTS tool results from feeding the automatic fallback media queue; TTS now returns its generated audio path in tool content for assistant-loop delivery
- document the two group-chat delivery modes: normal assistant MEDIA-line replies when agent-loop output is visible, and message-tool sends when explicit sends are required
- remove unrelated plugin-sdk cleanup from the PR scope

## Root cause
The prior fix still let system-owned media delivery compete with assistant-owned delivery. That avoided one mirror path, but kept generated media delivery outside the assistant turn and only covered async music/video.

## Fix
Generated image, music, video, and TTS audio now follow one model: tools return or wake with concrete media paths, then the assistant delivers those files according to the active chat mode. In group chats that forward normal assistant output, the assistant can reply with MEDIA lines. In group chats configured for explicit sends, the assistant uses the message tool and then returns `NO_REPLY`. Async music/video completions always wake the requester session with this delivery instruction; there is no direct-send bypass.

## Validation
- `pnpm vitest run src/agents/tools/video-generate-background.test.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-background.test.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.media.test.ts`
- `pnpm vitest run src/agents/tools/video-generate-background.test.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-background.test.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.media.test.ts src/config/config.schema-regressions.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:test`
- `pnpm format:check CHANGELOG.md docs/automation/tasks.md docs/gateway/config-tools.md src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.media.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.ts src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.ts src/agents/tools/media-generate-background.test-support.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-background.test.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-tool.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-background.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-tool.ts src/config/config.schema-regressions.test.ts src/config/schema.base.generated.ts src/config/schema.help.ts src/config/schema.labels.ts src/config/types.tools.ts src/config/zod-schema.core.ts`

target.title: fix(video): skip delivering tasks in active-task prompt guard
target.body:
Fixes #95701.

## What Problem This Solves
Sequential `video_generate` workflows blocked the next segment for ~4-5 min after each clip's video was ready. The active-task prompt guard treated the still-`running` task as blocking until the completion wake finished (self-deadlock), so crews producing clips 01, 02, 03 back-to-back paid a ~3.5-4 min "release tax" per segment on top of actual generation (~60-90s). The task only flipped `running -> succeeded` after the woken agent turn gave up and yielded.

## Evidence
- **Reporter production data** (issue #95701): `state/openclaw.sqlite` `task_runs` for one session - task `c6043e3f` ran 12:54:07 -> 12:59:25 (5m18s) with provider `byteplus/doubao-seedance`, where actual generation+download is ~60-90s. The requester trajectory shows the woken turn (12:54:35 -> 12:59:23, 4m48s) reporting "stale state machine, 5 retries intercepted", and the task flips to `succeeded` only at 12:59:25 - after the woken turn ended, not when the video was ready.
- **Root cause verified against current main** (`a182811070`): `scheduleMediaGenerationTaskCompletion` (`src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.ts:382`) gates `completeTaskRun` (:487) behind `wakeTaskCompletion(expectFinal:true)` (:431); the label-less prompt guard (`buildActiveMediaGenerationTaskPromptContextForSession` at `media-generation-task-status-shared.ts:447`) suppresses the next segment's `video_generate` while the task is still `running`.
- **Unit tests** (new + existing, 52 green): `listActive` excludes delivering tasks when the flag is set; `buildActive` returns no prompt when the only active task is delivering, still warns when mid-generation; existing video/image generation suites unchanged.

## Fix
- Extract the existing "Generated media; delivering completion" progress marker (set at `media-generate-background-shared.ts:418` since the wake gates `completeTaskRun`) into a shared constant `MEDIA_GENERATION_DELIVERING_COMPLETION_PROGRESS`.
- `listActiveMediaGenerationTasksForSession` / `findActiveMediaGenerationTaskForSession` gain an optional `excludeDeliveringCompletion` flag; the prompt guard `buildActiveMediaGenerationTaskPromptContextForSession` passes `true`, so a distinct next segment is no longer suppressed once media is ready.
- Duplicate guard (`findDuplicateGuardMediaGenerationTaskForSession`, already label-aware) and the `completeTaskRun` delivery-confirmation gate are **unchanged**.

## Scope / non-goals
- No change to the task status machine (no new `delivering` state), `TaskRecord` schema, duplicate guard, or the `completeTaskRun` safety contract ("generated result without confirmed delivery is terminally unsafe for closeout").

## Test plan
- [x] New unit tests (`media-generation-task-status-shared.test.ts`)
- [x] Existing suites green (52 tests)
- [ ] CI: `pnpm tsgo` + `pnpm check` (oxlint/oxfmt)
- [ ] Live regression (maintainer env, needs byteplus/LM Studio key): sequential `video_generate` of 2 distinct prompts - confirm 2nd isn't suppressed, task A flips to `succeeded` after the wake.

target.title: feat(config): support SecretRef for phone numbers in channel configs
target.body:
## Summary

Phone numbers are PII. If you're putting them in `allowFrom`, `defaultTo`, or `groupAllowFrom`, they're sitting in plaintext config. This PR adds SecretRef support for phone number fields across all messaging channels so those values can live in a secret provider instead.

- **Problem:** Phone numbers in channel configs (`allowFrom`, `defaultTo`, `groupAllowFrom`, `account`, `reactionAllowlist`) only accepted plain strings ŌĆö no way to keep them out of plaintext config files.
- **Why it matters:** Phone numbers are PII under GDPR/CCPA. SecretRef support lets users store them in env vars, files, or exec-based secret providers.
- **What changed:** WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, BlueBubbles, and voice-call channels now accept `SecretRef` objects for all phone number / sender identity fields. Exported TypeScript types, Zod schemas, secret contracts, runtime resolution, config UI hints, docs, and tests are all updated.
- **What did NOT change:** Existing plain string configs continue to work. No breaking changes to the config schema.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #56502
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A ŌĆö this is a new feature, not a bug fix.

- Root cause: N/A
- Missing detection / guardrail: N/A
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A ŌĆö this is a new feature. Tests are added for coverage, not regression.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `config-schema.test.ts` and `secret-contract.test.ts` for each channel, `zod-schema.secretref-allowfrom.test.ts`, `target-registry.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: SecretRef objects are accepted in phone number fields; invalid SecretRef shapes are rejected; `normalizeAllowFrom` filters out unresolved SecretRef objects instead of stringifying them to `[object Object]`; secret target registry discovers plugin manifest `secretInputs.paths`
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Schema-level tests catch accept/reject at the boundary; the `normalizeAllowFrom` test catches the runtime filtering; the target registry test catches manifest-driven secret discovery
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A ŌĆö new feature surface
- If no new test is added, why not: Tests are added

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `allowFrom`, `defaultTo`, `groupAllowFrom` (and Signal's `account`, `reactionAllowlist`) now accept SecretRef objects in addition to plain strings across WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and BlueBubbles.
- Voice-call's `fromNumber`, `toNumber`, and `allowFrom` accept SecretRef objects.
- Config UI hints mark these fields as `sensitive: true` so UIs mask them.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? Yes ŌĆö phone number fields can now be stored as SecretRefs
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: SecretRefs are resolved through the existing secret provider pipeline. No new secret resolution paths. `normalizeAllowFrom` filters out unresolved SecretRef objects so they can't leak as `[object Object]` strings into allowlists.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux (Devin VM)
- Runtime/container: Node 22, pnpm
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, BlueBubbles, voice-call
- Relevant config (redacted): `"allowFrom": [{ "source": "env", "provider": "default", "id": "MY_PHONE" }]`

### Steps

1. Add a SecretRef to a phone number field in config (e.g. `"allowFrom": [{ "source": "env", "provider": "default", "id": "MY_PHONE" }]`)
2. Run `openclaw doctor` or start the gateway
3. The SecretRef is resolved through the configured secret provider

### Expected

- Config parses successfully with SecretRef objects in phone number fields
- Plain strings continue to work as before

### Actual

- Confirmed via unit tests (73 tests across 8 test files, all passing)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

All tests pass: `pnpm tsgo` (typecheck), `pnpm check` (lint), `pnpm test` (73 relevant tests across schema validation, secret contracts, runtime normalization, and target registry).

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Schema acceptance/rejection for all 5 channels, `normalizeAllowFrom` SecretRef filtering, secret target registry manifest discovery, voice-call manifest path merging after rebase
- Edge cases checked: Invalid SecretRef shapes rejected, mixed arrays of strings + SecretRefs + numbers handled correctly, undefined input returns empty array, objects with custom `toString()` still work in `normalizeStringEntries`
- What I did **not** verify: End-to-end with a running gateway and real secret provider

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No (additive only ŌĆö existing configs unchanged)
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Unresolved SecretRef objects could reach `normalizeAllowFrom` at runtime
  - Mitigation: `normalizeAllowFrom` now filters out SecretRef objects via `isSecretRef()` before string normalization, preventing `[object Object]` entries in allowlists

---

**AI models used in this branch:**

GPT-5 (OpenAI Codex) via Codex
Claude Sonnet 4.6 (Anthropic) via Devin

target.title: fix(media): allow private openai compatible audio transcription endpoints
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Audio media-understanding transcription could target self-hosted OpenAI-compatible endpoints, but audio config did not expose the private-network request opt-in used by guarded provider HTTP.
- Why it matters: Trusted local, RFC1918, or tailnet Whisper-style endpoints need explicit private-network access without globally relaxing media request SSRF policy.
- What changed: Added audio-only request config support for `tools.media.audio.request.allowPrivateNetwork`, aligned schema/generated metadata/docs, threaded audio request overrides into provider execution, and aligned OpenAI-compatible loopback audio auth with the chat-completion local auth pattern by using the shared `custom-local` marker only for loopback OpenAI-compatible audio bases.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Image and video media request config still reject `allowPrivateNetwork`, private-network access remains disabled by default, explicit `request.auth` still injects the configured auth header, and media/audio does not add the Ollama-specific local-auth branch.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes N/A
- Related N/A
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Audio media config reused the generic media request schema/type path, which rejected `allowPrivateNetwork`, while provider execution needed an audio-specific trusted request override path. The local no-auth path also needed to follow the existing chat-completion pattern: synthesize the shared `custom-local` marker for loopback OpenAI-compatible endpoints, then avoid sending provider-default auth for that marker.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing schema and runner tests did not cover audio-specific private-network request overrides or loopback OpenAI-compatible audio endpoints without provider auth.
- Contributing context (if known): The config docs baseline and audio field metadata are generated/aligned surfaces for this config contract.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/config/schema.test.ts`, `src/media-understanding/openai-compatible-audio.test.ts`, `src/media-understanding/runner.proxy.test.ts`, `src/media-understanding/shared.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Audio config accepts and forwards `allowPrivateNetwork`, image/video reject it, per-model audio request config works, loopback OpenAI-compatible audio can synthesize `custom-local` when provider auth is absent, synthetic local audio auth sends no provider-default `Authorization`, explicit request auth still wins, and Ollama does not get the media/audio local-auth branch.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These tests exercise the config contract and provider request assembly without needing a live transcription service.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users can opt audio transcription requests into private-network endpoints with `tools.media.audio.request.allowPrivateNetwork` and per-model audio request config. Loopback OpenAI-compatible audio endpoints such as `http://127.0.0.1:8000/v1` can run without provider auth, matching local OpenAI-compatible chat-completion providers. The OpenAI provider docs now show the loopback example base URL.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: The new network capability is an explicit audio-only opt-in for trusted private endpoints; defaults remain blocked by the SSRF guard and image/video still reject the private-network flag. Local no-auth audio is restricted to loopback OpenAI-compatible audio providers using the existing non-secret `custom-local` marker. Explicit request auth still injects the configured header when present.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux 6.1.0-44-cloud-amd64 x86_64
- Runtime/container: Node v22.22.2, pnpm 10.33.0
- Model/provider: OpenAI-compatible audio transcription config path
- Integration/channel (if any): Media understanding audio attachments
- Relevant config (redacted): `tools.media.audio.request.allowPrivateNetwork`, `tools.media.audio.request.auth`, `tools.media.audio.models[].baseUrl`

### Steps

1. Parse audio, image, and video media config shapes through the runtime zod schema.
2. Run audio provider execution with provider-level, audio-level, and per-model request overrides.
3. Run provider HTTP request assembly with synthetic local auth and explicit request auth.

### Expected

- Audio request config accepts and forwards private-network opt-in only when explicitly configured.
- Image/video reject `allowPrivateNetwork` under media request config.
- Loopback OpenAI-compatible audio can use the shared synthetic local auth marker when provider auth is absent.
- Synthetic local auth does not become a provider-default `Authorization` header.
- Explicit request auth overrides synthetic local auth and injects the configured header.
- Ollama does not receive a media/audio local-auth special case.

### Actual

- Matches expected in local tests and changed-gate validation.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Local validation:

- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm test src/media-understanding/openai-compatible-audio.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.proxy.test.ts src/media-understanding/shared.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/model-auth.ts src/media-understanding/runner.entries.ts src/media-understanding/runner.proxy.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.test-mocks.ts docs/providers/openai.md docs/nodes/audio.md CHANGELOG.md src/media-understanding/openai-compatible-audio.ts src/media-understanding/openai-compatible-audio.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm check:changed`

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

Set up a (WhisperLive)[https://github.com/collabora/WhisperLive] server locally. Modified openclaw.json to point to it with `media.audio.request.allowPrivateNetwork = true`:

```
"media": {
      "audio": {
        "enabled": true,
        "language": "en",
        "models": [
          {
            "provider": "openai",
            "model": "whisper-1",
            "baseUrl": "http://127.0.0.1:8000/v1",
          }
        ],
        "request": {
          "allowPrivateNetwork": true,
        }
      }
```

Verified audio transcription:

```
$ openclaw capability audio transcribe --file tmp/media-test/transcription-test.wav --language en --json
{
  "ok": true,
  "capability": "audio.transcribe",
  "transport": "local",
  "attempts": [],
  "outputs": [
    {
      "path": "/home/openclaw/.openclaw/workspace/tmp/media-test/transcription-test.wav",
      "text": "Open Claw Media Transcription Test. The passphrase is Brass Lobster 7.",
      "kind": "audio.transcription"
    }
  ]
}
```



## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

No PR review conversations currently exist.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes, optional new audio request config only
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Users could point audio transcription at private endpoints they do not fully trust.
  - Mitigation: The private-network exception is explicit, audio-only, disabled by default, documented as trusted-endpoint-only, and covered by schema/runtime tests.
- Risk: Synthetic local auth could accidentally be sent as provider-default auth.
  - Mitigation: OpenAI-compatible audio now uses the shared chat-completion local auth marker and omits provider-default auth for that marker while preserving explicit request auth overrides.

target.title: fix(media): resolve absolute-path channel media for native vision blocks
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Telegram inbound images saved to disk are not passed to the model as native vision content blocks. The model receives only `<media:image>` placeholder text and cannot analyze the actual image. The same workflow works correctly through the webchat / Control UI.
- Why now: Any user sending images via Telegram DM to an OpenClaw bot with a vision-capable model cannot get the agent to see or describe the image. The image file is correctly downloaded to `~/.openclaw/media/inbound/` but is silently dropped during native vision block hydration.
- What changed:
  - `MediaAttachmentCache.resolveLocalPath()` now validates absolute filesystem paths against local roots via `resolveUsableLocalCandidate`, matching the existing relative-path validation added in 610691c796
  - `resolveCurrentTurnImages()` now returns successfully resolved images even when a subset fails, instead of discarding the entire batch
  - Added regression test for absolute-path media hydration
- What did NOT change: Relative-path handling is unchanged. Webchat inline-image path (early-return on `params.images`) is untouched. No new dependencies added.
- Outcome: Channel-delivered absolute-path images (Telegram, Discord, Slack, etc.) are now hydrated into native vision content blocks instead of being silently dropped on a single read failure.
- Reviewer focus: `attachments.cache.ts` changes to `resolveLocalPath`, `current-turn-images.ts` changes to the all-or-nothing fallback logic, and the new test case.

## Linked context

closes #93816 Closes #79162, closes #62292, closes #69808

Was this requested by a maintainer?
- No, but the issues have clear reproduction steps and root cause analysis.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `resolveLocalPath()` skipped absolute-path validation ŌĆö it returned `path.resolve(rawPath)` directly while relative paths went through `resolveUsableLocalCandidate`. Channel-delivered inbound media (Telegram, Discord, Slack, etc.) use absolute filesystem paths from `saveMediaBuffer`. When the path later failed `isInboundPathAllowed` in `ensureLocalStat`, the error was silently caught ŌĆö channel attachments have `url` cleared, so `getBuffer` had no fallback and re-threw. Separately, `resolveCurrentTurnImages()` discarded all images when any single read failed.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test coverage for absolute-path media hydration. The existing test only covered relative paths. The symmetric relative-path validation in `resolveLocalPath` (added in 610691c796) was the guardrail that should have also covered absolute paths.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram inbound images saved to disk are not passed to the model as native vision content blocks; the model receives only `<media:image>` placeholder text and cannot analyze the actual image.
- Real environment tested: Ubuntu 24.04 (x64), Node 22.22.0, OpenClaw main branch (9166165).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```bash
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/current-turn-images.test.ts
  ```
- Evidence after fix:
  ```text
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs output:
  Ō£ō hydrates Telegram-style state-relative media into native prompt images
  Ō£ō hydrates channel-delivered absolute-path media into native prompt images
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  2 passed (18)
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The new absolute-path test passes alongside the existing relative-path test. resolveCurrentTurnImages now returns partially-resolved image batches instead of discarding all images on a single failure. The absolute-path validation in resolveLocalPath is symmetric with the existing relative-path handling.
- What was not tested: Not run against a live Telegram bot with a real image. The proof exercises the full hydration chain through the media store with local file I/O.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Unit-test-based ŌĆö does not verify a live end-to-end Telegram roundtrip.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/current-turn-images.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/media-understanding/runner.attachments.test.ts`

What regression coverage was added or updated?
- Added 1 new test case:
  1. `hydrates channel-delivered absolute-path media into native prompt images` ŌĆö writes a file under a temp state/media directory, sets `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and asserts absolute-path hydration into native `ImageContent` blocks

What failed before this fix, if known?
- Telegram inbound images appeared only as `<media:image>` placeholder text
- Any channel using `saveMediaBuffer` for inbound media could silently lose native vision blocks when a single attachment read failed

­¤ż¢ Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(exec): decode Windows command output with codepage-aware streaming
target.body:
## Summary
- Fix Windows exec output mojibake for CJK locales by decoding captured command output with the active Windows code page instead of assuming UTF-8.
- Preserve decoder state across stdout/stderr chunks or decode buffered byte streams so split GBK/Shift_JIS characters are not replaced.
- Add focused regression coverage for CP936/GBK Chinese output and split multibyte chunks.

## Credit
This carries forward the useful approach from @slepybear's source PR https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/64661, which could not be safely updated directly because the branch is closed, maintainer_can_modify=false, checks failed, and bot review identified unresolved decoder-state issues.

## Linked Issues
Fixes/addresses the Windows CJK exec mojibake family reported in #40613, #50519, and #56462.

## Validation
- pnpm -s vitest run src/infra/windows-encoding.test.ts src/process/exec.windows.test.ts

target.title: feat(tasks): improve task state transitions and lifecycle formatting
target.body:
## Summary
- add explicit intermediate active task states for approval waits and external waits
- propagate those active-state semantics across registry maintenance, flow guards, CLI views, and auto-reply task rendering
- add compressed operational summaries plus shared task lifecycle event formatting for state-change updates
- reuse the shared lifecycle formatter in task update delivery paths and extend focused task coverage

## Why
Previously, tasks moved through coarse active states like `queued` and `running`, which made it hard to distinguish between:
- work that is actively executing
- work that is alive but blocked on user approval
- work that is alive but waiting on an external dependency

This keeps the task model incremental while making the lifecycle more explicit and observable across the main task consumers.

## Notes
- intermediate task states are still projected as running at the `TaskFlow` layer for compatibility
- this PR also adds compressed operational summaries and lifecycle-oriented state-change formatting to make task progress easier to inspect across surfaces

## Testing
- OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test:fast -- src/tasks/task-status.test.ts src/tasks/task-executor-policy.test.ts
- OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test:fast -- src/tasks/task-status.test.ts src/tasks/task-executor-policy.test.ts src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts src/tasks/task-registry.store.test.ts
- existing focused task-state tests retained in the PR history for intermediate active state semantics

target.title: fix(safeguard): resolve compaction provider/model before registering runtime
target.body:
Fixes #57901.

## Summary

Two-part fix for the safeguard auto-compaction path ignoring `agents.defaults.compaction.model`:

1. **`extensions.ts`**: `buildEmbeddedExtensionFactories` now reuses the same compaction target resolver as `compaction-runtime-context.ts`, so the safeguard runtime gets the resolved compaction provider/model/auth instead of the active session model.
2. **`compaction-safeguard.ts`**: swaps precedence in the hook from `ctx.model ?? runtime?.model` to `runtime?.model ?? ctx.model`. Configured compaction model now wins when present; falls back to session model when no override is set.

## Why the previous PR (#57903) wasn't enough

Per the maintainer Codex review on this issue, the explicit `/compact` and context-engine compaction paths were already correct on main. The closed PR #57903 only swapped the precedence in the hook. The remaining bug ŌĆö and what this PR fixes ŌĆö is the embedded-extension runtime, which was still being populated with the active session model at registration time. So even with the precedence swap, the safeguard runtime had the wrong `runtime?.model` to compare against.

## Tests

**769 tests pass, 0 errors, 0 failed.**

New regression: `extensions.test.ts` and `compaction-safeguard.test.ts` cover the case where `ctx.model` = session model (e.g., Opus) and `agents.defaults.compaction.model` = different (e.g., Sonnet). Asserts the configured compaction model wins.

Maintainer review bar (per [comment 4336639752](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/57901#issuecomment-4336639752)) ŌĆö all four guardrails covered:
- `/compact` not regressed
- Context-engine path not regressed
- Auth-profile dropping when providers change still works
- No-override fallback (when `agents.defaults.compaction.model` is unset) still falls back to session model

## Stats

- CHANGELOG.md untouched (release generation owns it per repo policy)

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Safeguard auto-compaction was ignoring `agents.defaults.compaction.model`: the embedded-extension runtime was registered with the active session model regardless of any configured compaction override (#57901). This PR makes `buildEmbeddedExtensionFactories` call `resolveSafeguardRuntimeTarget`, which routes through `resolveEmbeddedCompactionTarget` + `resolveModelWithRegistry`, and flips the precedence in the hook to `runtime?.model ?? ctx.model`. Inline-only models defined under `models.providers.*.models` are honored, and a misconfigured/missing override now triggers a `log.warn(...)` instead of silently registering `model: undefined`.

**Real environment tested:** exe.dev VM, `openclaw/openclaw` fork `joeykrug/openclaw`, worktree at `.../pr-73704-58823/pr-73704`, branch `fix/57901-safeguard-compaction-model` at HEAD `a7e454ae59b3850786ccf15cf9f9e2ec0d779948` (after merging `upstream/main` into the PR branch). Node `v24.15.0`, pnpm `11.1.0`. Sources read live from `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/extensions.ts`, `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compaction-runtime-context.ts`, `src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard-runtime.ts`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

1. `pnpm install --prefer-offline --ignore-scripts` (worktree bootstrap, `SHARP_IGNORE_GLOBAL_LIBVIPS=1`).
2. Added a standalone real-behavior proof at `scripts/proof/pr-73704-compaction-model.mjs` that imports the actual patched source ŌĆö `buildEmbeddedExtensionFactories`, `resolveSafeguardRuntimeTarget`, `resolveEmbeddedCompactionTarget`, and `getCompactionSafeguardRuntime` ŌĆö and exercises three scenarios end-to-end (no vitest, no mocks of the function under test).
3. Ran: `node --import tsx scripts/proof/pr-73704-compaction-model.mjs` from the worktree root. Captured terminal output verbatim below.
4. Also ran the focused regression gates against the merged tree: `pnpm tsgo:core` (EXIT=0), `pnpm tsgo:core:test` (EXIT=0), `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/extensions.test.ts src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.auth-profile-propagation.test.ts` (Tests: 99 passed across 3 files), `pnpm lint:core` (0 warnings, 0 errors).

**Evidence after fix:** terminal stdout from the live `node --import tsx scripts/proof/pr-73704-compaction-model.mjs` run against the patched source (exit code 0):

```text
$ node --import tsx scripts/proof/pr-73704-compaction-model.mjs
--- Scenario A: configured compaction model overrides session model ---
  session provider/model in : anthropic/claude-opus-4-7
  configured compaction      : anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 (from agents.defaults.compaction.model)
  resolveEmbeddedCompactionTarget => provider="anthropic" model="claude-sonnet-4-6"
  OK   pure target provider = "anthropic"
  OK   pure target model = "claude-sonnet-4-6"
    modelRegistry.find("anthropic", "claude-sonnet-4-6")
  resolveSafeguardRuntimeTarget => provider="anthropic" modelId="claude-sonnet-4-6" model.id="claude-sonnet-4-6"
  OK   runtime target provider = "anthropic"
  OK   runtime target modelId = "claude-sonnet-4-6"
  OK   runtime target model.id = "claude-sonnet-4-6"
  OK   runtime target model.provider = "anthropic"
  OK   runtime target model.id is NOT session id = true
    modelRegistry.find("anthropic", "claude-sonnet-4-6")
  registered safeguard runtime  : provider="anthropic" model.id="claude-sonnet-4-6" contextWindowTokens=200000
  OK   registered runtime model.id = "claude-sonnet-4-6"
  OK   registered runtime model.provider = "anthropic"
  OK   registered runtime model.id != session model id = true

--- Scenario B: no compaction.model configured -> session model preserved ---
  registered safeguard runtime  : model.id="claude-opus-4-7" (session id was "claude-opus-4-7")
  OK   session model.id preserved when no override = "claude-opus-4-7"
  OK   registered runtime model is session model (no override) = true

--- Scenario C: configured compaction model missing from registry ---
[agent/embedded] Configured safeguard compaction model "anthropic/claude-typo-4-6" could not be resolved; falling back to the session model.
  unresolved target             : provider="anthropic" modelId="claude-typo-4-6" model=undefined (safeguard surfaces miss)
  OK   provider stays as configured = "anthropic"
  OK   modelId stays as configured = "claude-typo-4-6"
  OK   model resolves to undefined on miss = undefined

PROOF PASSED: configured compaction model wins over session model in safeguard registration.
```

Supplementary CI gates against the merged tree (all green):

```text
$ pnpm tsgo:core
EXIT=0  (no diagnostics)

$ pnpm tsgo:core:test
EXIT=0  (no diagnostics)

$ pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/extensions.test.ts src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.auth-profile-propagation.test.ts
 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  99 passed (99)

$ pnpm lint:core
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
```

**Observed result after fix:** the live `node --import tsx` proof script terminates with `PROOF PASSED` and exit code 0. Scenario A: with `agents.defaults.compaction.model = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6"` and session model `anthropic/claude-opus-4-7`, the safeguard runtime registered on the SessionManager carries `model.id = "claude-sonnet-4-6"` and `model.provider = "anthropic"` ŌĆö the configured compaction model, not the session model. Scenario B: with no override, the safeguard runtime keeps the session model (`claude-opus-4-7`). Scenario C: with a missing/typo override (`anthropic/claude-typo-4-6`), `resolveSafeguardRuntimeTarget` returns `model: undefined`, the runtime carries the configured provider/modelId, and `[agent/embedded] Configured safeguard compaction model ... could not be resolved; falling back to the session model.` is emitted on stderr/stdout. Behavior matches the precedence inverted in `compaction-safeguard.ts` (`const model = runtime?.model ?? ctx.model;`).

**What was not tested:** no live provider API call was made ŌĆö proof stops at safeguard runtime registration + target resolution against the actual patched source. The end-to-end "real compaction summary served by the configured model over the wire" requires a long-running session with an active provider account; that was not run in this VM. The script also does not exercise the `extensions.test.ts` greptile P1 path through vitest (covered separately by the existing test file and the supplementary `pnpm test` run above).

target.title: Redesign Control UI channels directory
target.body: ## What Problem This Solves The Control UI Channels page was hard to scan because every channel rendered as a full card and the raw status snapshot was always visible. That made it harder to quickly tell which channels are enabled, disabled, or need attention. ## Why This Change Was Made This redesign turns Channels into a filterable directory with compact rows, channel logos, status dots, and expandable details. The detailed status/config content still exists, but it now lives inside each expanded row, while the raw Gateway snapshot is tucked behind a disclosure. It also keeps expanded channel rows in the URL query string so a focused channel view can survive reloads or be shared. ## User Impact Users can scan channel status faster, filter by enabled or disabled channels, expand only the channel they are working on, and still access the existing per-channel configuration controls. Collapsed rows also surface summary and account-level errors through the warning status dot. ## Evidence - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/channels.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-channels.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/channels.test.ts ui/src/ui/navigation.test.ts` - `pnpm tsgo:test:ui` - `pnpm ui:i18n:check` - `pnpm ui:build` passed with the existing large chunk warning - `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` clean: no accepted/actionable findings Images: Before : After Full `pnpm check && pnpm test` was not run; validation was targeted to the Control UI channels surface. target.title: fix(issue-template): split logs and screenshots into separate fields
target.body:
## What

Splits the **Logs, screenshots, and evidence** field in the bug report issue template into two separate fields:

- **Logs** - retains `render: shell` for syntax highlighting
- **Screenshots, recordings, and evidence** - a plain textarea that accepts image attachments

## Why

The original field described itself as accepting logs, screenshots, and recordings, but `render: shell` forces the textarea into code-block mode which silently prevents image attachments. Users dragging or pasting a screenshot into that field would find it never attaches.

The field was trying to do two incompatible things at once. Splitting them fixes the screenshot UX without losing shell syntax highlighting for logs.

## Change

```diff
  - type: textarea
    id: logs
    attributes:
-     label: Logs, screenshots, and evidence
-     description: Include the redacted logs, screenshots, recordings, docs, or version comparisons that support the grounded answers above.
+     label: Logs
+     description: Include the redacted logs or version comparisons that support the grounded answers above.
      render: shell
+ - type: textarea
+   id: screenshots_evidence
+   attributes:
+     label: Screenshots, recordings, and evidence
+     description: Include screenshots, recordings, docs, or other evidence that support the grounded answers above.
```
## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Screenshots and images pasted into the "Logs, screenshots, and evidence" field silently fail to attach because `render: shell` disables file attachment support in GitHub issue forms.

- **Real environment tested**: https://github.com/d1rshan/openclaw ŌĆö fork with fix applied to default branch, tested via GitHub's live issue form UI.

- **Exact steps or command run after fix**:
  1. Opened a new issue on the fork at https://github.com/d1rshan/openclaw/issues/new/choose
  2. Selected the bug report template
  3. Dragged a screen recording into the new "Screenshots, recordings, and evidence" field
  4. Confirmed the recording attached successfully (markdown attachment link appeared in the field)

- **Evidence after fix**: 

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/12a0965c-0fac-43ea-aded-ab4c6cd1368e

- **Observed result after fix**: Recording attached successfully to the "Screenshots, recordings, and evidence" field. The "Logs" field retains shell syntax highlighting as expected.

- **What was not tested**: None

- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

target.title: docs(rfd): @openclaw/call-sdk ŌĆö provider-agnostic real-time call SDK
target.body:
## What

Adds an RFC + two companion design docs proposing **`@openclaw/call-sdk`** ŌĆö a provider-agnostic real-time call SDK ŌĆö and its binding into OpenClaw's `ChannelPlugin` model.

The three docs are intentionally split so each can be reviewed independently:

| File | What |
|---|---|
| [`docs/refactor/call-sdk-rfd.mdx`](https://github.com/visionik/clawdis/blob/rfc/call-sdk/docs/refactor/call-sdk-rfd.mdx) | RFC summary: goal, non-goals, proposed interface sketch, three hard tradeoffs. Entry point that links to the two companion docs. |
| [`docs/refactor/call-sdk-design.mdx`](https://github.com/visionik/clawdis/blob/rfc/call-sdk/docs/refactor/call-sdk-design.mdx) | Portable SDK API design: `Call`, `CallTransport`, `CallTap` and built-in tap kinds (`InferenceTap`, `RecordingTap`, `CaptionTap`, `IVRTap`, `TransformTap`, `MixerTap`, `TestTap`), `MediaFrame` v1ŌåÆv2 plan, fan-out / `AbortSignal` as v1 core, lifecycle observability events, v1 / v1.1 / v2 / later scope split, worked examples, phased roadmap. |
| [`docs/refactor/call-sdk-channel-binding.mdx`](https://github.com/visionik/clawdis/blob/rfc/call-sdk/docs/refactor/call-sdk-channel-binding.mdx) | OpenClaw-side binding: `capabilities.call`, `ChannelPlugin.call: ChannelCallAdapter`, the load-bearing "channels never tap" guardrail (enforced at types / runtime / PR docs), authority/trust boundary between channels and tap-attaching code, two-axes / two-registries split, session-key conventions with `:call:<callId>`, `channel-call-inbound` dispatch helpers, single-connection contract, real-world capability matrix, 8-phase migration path. |

You can also browse all three in the [Files changed tab](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/73606/files).

## Why

OpenClaw handles dozens of messaging channels uniformly today. Real-time calls are not ŌĆö every transport (WhatsApp calling, the in-progress Google Meet integration, Twilio voice) has its own ad-hoc wiring, no shared `Call` lifecycle, no standard way to attach STT/TTS, no recording, no captions. Every new transport reinvents the same patterns.

This RFC proposes the missing shared layer: a portable `@openclaw/call-sdk` exposing `Call` (per-call lifecycle and media surface) and `CallTap` (anything that consumes or produces media on a call), plus a thin OpenClaw binding (`ChannelPlugin.call: ChannelCallAdapter`) so a single channel package per platform can own both messaging and calls cleanly.

## Three hard tradeoffs (decided)

1. **`Buffer` v1, `MediaFrame` v2.** Ship `AsyncIterable<Buffer>` now to unblock the abstractions; design `MediaFrame` (codec / sample-rate / sequence / track / EOS / error) against real codec needs in v2.
2. **Multi-tap composition over single-bridge.** Many `CallTap`s per call, independent. Requires fan-out + `AbortSignal` cancellation to be boringly correct in v1 core, not v1.1 polish ŌĆö otherwise detach leaks pipelines.
3. **Policy lives in the host, not the SDK.** `attachTap(call, tap)` is a plain function. OpenClaw wraps it as `core.attachTap(...)` under RBAC; the SDK exposes only attach/detach observability events for hosts to wire consent, audit, and recording-disclosure on top.

## Scope of this PR

**Docs only.** No code, no package added. The RFC asks for review of the design before any implementation begins; companion docs spell out the v1 / v1.1 / v2 / later split so reviewers can see the implementation-phase shape without that work being part of this PR.

## Open questions worth surfacing at RFD level

- Should `messaging` become optional on `ChannelPlugin` so call-only platforms (Meet, PSTN) are first-class? (Recommended: yes.)
- Should the plugin-sdk module path be `channel-call` (matches the renamed cap and SDK) or stay `channel-voice` (matches existing `realtime-voice` registry vocabulary)? (Recommended: `channel-call`.)

## External draft references

These docs were synthesized from in-progress drafts; gists kept until the RFD is merged for reviewers who want the change history:

- Call-SDK design gist: https://gist.github.com/visionik/e95177cc29fe5f9b8d7bb983635a520c
- Channel-plugin design gist: https://gist.github.com/visionik/6d27c758552403ea4b4641c72d73ebda

---

target.title: fix(agents): load symlinked workspace bootstrap files
target.body:
## Summary

- Keep the fallback scoped to fixed bootstrap files and preserve existing protections for hardlinks, non-regular files, dangling symlinks, oversized files, and extra/glob bootstrap paths.
